members.name: IsInputLoopBrokenAtEnd
members.summary: Returns whether the animation breaks the loop at the end of the loop cycle for transition.

members.name: IsInputReset
members.summary: Returns whether the animation restarts when the animation transitions from the other animation.

members.name: IsInputSetAsAutoAdvance
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if auto-advance is enabled for the given [param input] index.

members.name: SetInputAsAutoAdvance
members.signature: public void SetInputAsAutoAdvance(int input, bool enable)
members.summary: Enables or disables auto-advance for the given [param input] index. If enabled, state changes to the next input after playing the animation once. If enabled for the last input state, it loops to the first.

members.name: SetInputBreakLoopAtEnd
members.signature: public void SetInputBreakLoopAtEnd(int input, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], breaks the loop at the end of the loop cycle for transition, even if the animation is looping.

members.name: SetInputReset
members.signature: public void SetInputReset(int input, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the destination animation is restarted when the animation transitions.

members.name: AllowTransitionToSelf
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], allows transition to the self state. When the reset option is enabled in input, the animation is restarted. If [code]false[/code], nothing happens on the transition to the self state.

members.name: InputCount
members.summary: The number of enabled input ports for this animation node.

members.name: XfadeCurve
members.summary: Determines how cross-fading between animations is eased. If empty, the transition will be linear. Should be a unit [Curve].

members.name: XfadeTime
members.summary: Cross-fading time (in seconds) between each animation connected to the inputs. [b]Note:[/b] [AnimationNodeTransition] transitions the current state immediately after the start of the fading. The precise remaining time can only be inferred from the main animation. When [AnimationNodeOutput] is considered as the most upstream, so the [member xfade_time] is not scaled depending on the downstream delta. See also [member AnimationNodeOneShot.fadeout_time].
